topic: Taiwan President Urges Party to Oppose China's 'Red Terror' Tactics
headline: TAIWAN PRESIDENT URGES PARTY TO OPPOSE CHINA'S 'RED TERROR' TACTICS, SITUATION REMAINS TENSE WITH MILITARY ACTIVITY ON THE RISE
summary: Taiwan's President-elect William Lai Ching-te has won the election, China has reiterated that reunification is 'inevitable', and Taiwan's president is urging his party to oppose China's tactics. The situation remains tense with heightened military activity and ongoing political discussions.

summary_sources.title: Taiwan ruling party’s Lai wins presidential election | Elections News | Al Jazeera
Taiwan president calls for peace and dialogue with China amid heightened military activity | Taiwan | The Guardian
World reacts to Taiwan election as China says reunification ...

sections.paragraph: China's President Xi Jinping has stated that the unification of China and Taiwan is 'inevitable', with the use of military force remaining an option. This stance is part of a long-standing claim by China that Taiwan is a breakaway province that will eventually be under Beijing's control. The two sides have been separately ruled since the Chinese civil war of the 1940s, with Taiwan enjoying de facto independence. Historically, China has offered alternatives to military action, such as the 'one country, two systems' concept proposed by Deng Xiaoping in 1979, but Taiwan has maintained its own distinct identity, with a constitution and democratically-elected leaders. The status of Taiwan remains a point of tension, with China's threat to invade considered higher than ever, while Taiwan sees itself as a separate entity with its own government and international links. The international community is also divided, with fewer than 15 world governments recognizing Taiwan as a country.

sections.paragraph: The recent presidential election in Taiwan has significant implications for the island's relationship with China, with Beijing having long claimed Taiwan as a breakaway province. The election saw William Lai Ching-te from the governing Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) win, despite warnings from China not to vote for him. China has stepped up military pressure on Taiwan in recent years, with Chinese President Xi Jinping stating that the "unification" of Taiwan with China is "inevitable". The DPP's win may increase tensions with China, which has accused the party of not representing mainstream public opinion. The opposition parties in Taiwan, including the Kuomintang (KMT), have also been at odds with the DPP, and have been accused of blocking Lai's agenda. Lai has offered national security briefings to the KMT in an effort to promote dialogue and stability.

sections.paragraph: Chinese President Xi Jinping has vowed to reunify China and Taiwan, stating that reunification is 'unstoppable' in his New Year's Eve speech. This statement was made after China concluded intense military drills around Taiwan, which included live-fire exercises simulating a blockade of the island. The drills and Xi's speech have been seen as a 'stern warning' against 'separatist' and 'external interference' forces. China claims Taiwan as part of its territory and has long vowed to annex it, using force if necessary. The situation has been exacerbated by recent provocative actions by Chinese forces in the seas separating the two regions, including sending military flotillas and flybys towards Taiwan. While analysts believe an invasion is not likely, Chinese state media have highlighted China's military might, emphasizing that the direction of the situation across the Taiwan Strait is determined by a 'contest of strength'.

sections.paragraph: Mao Zedong's history is marked by significant events and writings that shaped his role in Chinese politics. During the fight against Japanese brutality, the Red Army, led by Mao, grew from 50,000 to 500,000 members and initiated the Hundred Regiments Offensive, a military success that resulted in the death of 20,000 Japanese. Mao also authored several texts, including Philosophy of Revolution and Protracted Warfare, which offered introductions to Marxist theory and guerrilla warfare. In the 1960s, Mao proposed the Socialist Education Movement and published Quotations from Chairman Mao Tse-tung, known as the Little Red Book, which contained short extracts from his speeches and articles. Additionally, Mao's writings are compiled in the Selected Works of Mao Zedong, although there has never been an official Complete Works of Mao Zedong. Mao's early career was also marked by his involvement in the May 4, 1919, demonstration and his editorial writings, which showcased his generation's determination to take responsibility for the country's fate.
